rows.snippet: List of Changes for Quick Fields 10.1 3 years ago fail with "Could not find part of path" I/O errors. (150071) Quick Fields Scanning now formats date and time fields according to the computer region setting. (149922) (147605) Quick .... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013824/list-of-changes-for-quick-fields- ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Quick Fields 10.1
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Quick Fields 10.1
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Quick Fields 10.1 Product: Laserfiche Quick Fields 10.1 KB: 1013824 Published: February 17, 2017 Last Updated: November 7, 2022 Summary This article documents the new features and changes for Quick Fields 10.1. New Features The new multi-value field groups in Laserfiche 10.2 are supported in Quick Fields and Quick Fields Scanning. Arabic and Thai are now supported in any Quick Fields process that contains OCR (OmniPage OCR and OmniPage Zone OCR). These languages are licensed separately. You can now manually input tokens into the pattern matching text box of the Auto-Annotation process to account for patterns that are different per document. Changes Local users are now supported and can be added in the Quick Fields Administration Console on the Quick Fields Server machine. (145758) (149709) Barcode now supports the +, $, % encoded characters in Extended Code 39. (150839) Some processes that previously caused Quick Fields Scanning to misidentify documents no longer misidentify documents. (150079) Sessions running in Quick Fields Agent no longer randomly fail with "Could not find part of path" I/O errors. (150071) Quick Fields Scanning now formats date and time fields according to the computer region setting. (149922) (147605) Quick Fields tokens now successfully resolve in version comments in Quick Fields Scanning. (149884) (144897) Disabled processes will no longer incorrectly run in Quick Fields Scanning. (149800) Using tokens in number fields no longer causes errors when storing all the documents at once from Quick Fields Scanning. (149652) A disabled document class no longer gets re-enabled after you publish it and then reopen it from the Quick Fields Server. (149450) If there are multiple identification conditions where "All" must be true, Quick Fields Scanning no longer ignores all but the first one. (149437) Zone OCR processes in sessions migrated from Quick Fields 7 now run properly in Quick Fields Scanning 10. (149396) You will no longer receive occasional out of bounds errors when using the rotate option inside a Zone OCR process. (148498) The Quick Fields Server Configuration Utility's validator has been updated to allow period characters for domain and local user accounts. (148491) Barcode regions using percentage units now read barcodes correctly. (148311) The Barcode process can now read 128 barcodes in Quick Fields Scanning. (146439) Deleting local enhancements now successfully marks a document as modified in the Publish Session dialog box. (146437) Publishing changes to a session or document class more than once now publishes successfully. (149799) (150160) In Quick Fields Agent, a session using Laserfiche Capture Engine with a non named user account to retrieve documents no longer returns an error. (118811) The advanced option in the Zone OCR process to mark a field based on confidence level now successfully marks the field in Quick Fields Scanning. (149177) A "Given key was not present in the dictionary" error no longer erroneously appears when sessions are run with Quick Fields Agent. (151540) Duplicate files types are no longer listed in the File Type list in the Universal Capture dialog box in Quick Fields and Quick Fields Scanning. (150635) Quick Fields Scanning now always opens as an administrator and no longer throws access restriction errors. (154291, 154290) If "Login required" is configured for a document class' authentication method, Windows authentication is no longer automatically used to authenticate. (154174) In Quick Fields Scanning, you will now be prompted to delete a document you remove all pages from. (150552) The metadata updater now closes successfully and does not cause Quick Fields to freeze. (144741) Related Links 1013823 Release Notes for Quick Fields 10.1 Quick Fields Help Files 1013788 List of Changes for Quick Fields 10 Update 1 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object." error when creating a new licensing site 5 years ago Object reference not set to an instance of an object." error when creating a new licensing site September 30, 2020 | KB: 1014206 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014206/-quot-object ... ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014206/object-reference-not-set-to-an-in ...
rows.title: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object." error when creating a new licensing site
rows.detailTitle: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object." error when creating a new licensing site
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith "Object reference not set to an instance of an object." error when creating a new licensing site Product: Directory Server 10.4.5 KB: 1014206 Published: September 29, 2020 Last Updated: September 30, 2020 Summary When creating a new licensing site in Directory Server 10.4.5, you may see an Object reference not set to an instance of an object. error. Cause Licensing site creation takes a long time and displays an Object reference not set to an instance of an object. error on the New Licensing Site page. This will occur in systems where Directory Server has a slow connection to the domain controllers of any trusted domain, causing Directory Server to become unresponsive during this extended site creation period. Resolution Use the following workaround to successfully create a licensing site: In Directory Server, click the Create Site button. Ignore the Object reference not set to an instance of an object. error that appears. Wait for an event to be recorded in the Laserfiche-Directory Service-Server Operational trace logs that looks like Mounted database [myExampleSite]. This may take several minutes depending on your network and domain setup. Return to your browser and refresh the page. You may now use the new licensing site. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An "Error determining word locations. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Applying Linked Text Annotations 20 years ago An "Error determining word locations. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Applying Linked Text Annotations September 14, 2005 | KB: 1011038 https://sup... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011038/an-error-determining-word- ...
rows.title: An "Error determining word locations. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Applying Linked Text Annotations
rows.detailTitle: An "Error determining word locations. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Applying Linked Text Annotations
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Error determining word locations. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Applying Linked Text Annotations Product: Server (MSDE) 7, Server (MSSQL) 7 KB: 1011038 Published: September 14, 2005 Last Updated: September 14, 2005 Summary When applying a linked text highlight or linked text redaction, you may receive the following error message: Error determining word locations. Error executing SQL command [7050] Cause This error can occur when an expansion database is not correctly attached, or when it has been attached under an incorrect name. Expansion databases are created, when using Microsoft SQL Server Desktop Edition (MSDE) and Microsoft SQL Server: Personal Edition (MSSQL:PE) installations, to store word location information. Word location information is used to associate a text highlight or redaction with the correct place on the image. If an expansion database is not attached, or is attached under a different name, Laserfiche will be unable to access word location information. Resolution Expansion databases must be attached to the SQL server. For instructions on correctly attaching your expansion database, please see the following Laserfiche Knowledge Base article: 1011022 HOW TO: Attaching Expansion Databases to MSDE or SQL Server Personal Edition. More Information For more information about expansion database files, please see the following Laserfiche Knowledge Base article: 1000519: INFO: MSDE and SQL Server Personal Edition Database File Information. For other issues with the same root cause and resolution, please see the following Laserfiche Knowledge Base articles: 1011015 ERROR: A "Failed to load thumbnails. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Opening a Document. 1011023 ERROR: A "Failed writing data to repository [7050]" Error Occurs When OCRing. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
